From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

1914 film

This article is about the 1914 British film. For the American film from the same year, see [A Study in Scarlet (1914 American film)](/wiki/A_Study_in_Scarlet_(1914_American_film) "A Study in Scarlet (1914 American film)").

| A Study in Scarlet | |
| --- | --- |
| James Bragington as Sherlock Holmes | |
| Directed by | [George Pearson](/wiki/George_Pearson_(filmmaker) "George Pearson (filmmaker)") |
| Written by | [Arthur Conan Doyle](/wiki/Arthur_Conan_Doyle "Arthur Conan Doyle") (novel) [Harry Engholm](/wiki/Harry_Engholm "Harry Engholm") |
| Produced by | George Pearson [G. B. Samuelson](/wiki/G._B._Samuelson "G. B. Samuelson") |
| Starring | James Bragington |
| Cinematography | Walter Buckstone |
| Release date | * 28 December 1914 (1914-12-28) |
| Running time | 6 reels |
| Country | United Kingdom |
| Language | Silent with English intertitles |

***A Study in Scarlet*** is a 1914 British [silent](/wiki/Silent_film "Silent film") [drama film](/wiki/Drama_film "Drama film") directed by [George Pearson](/wiki/George_Pearson_(filmmaker) "George Pearson (filmmaker)") and starring James Bragington,[[1]](#cite_note-bostrom155-1) making him the first English actor to portray Holmes on film.[[1]](#cite_note-bostrom155-1) It is based on the Sir [Arthur Conan Doyle](/wiki/Arthur_Conan_Doyle "Arthur Conan Doyle") 1887 novel [of the same name](/wiki/A_Study_in_Scarlet "A Study in Scarlet") and is considered to be [lost](/wiki/Lost_film "Lost film").[[2]](#cite_note-silentera-2)[[3]](#cite_note-bioscope-3) [An American film of the same name](/wiki/A_Study_in_Scarlet_(1914_US_film) "A Study in Scarlet (1914 US film)") was released in the U.S. on the following day, 29 December 1914. As of 2014[[update]](https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=A_Study_in_Scarlet_(1914_British_film)&action=edit), the film is missing from the [BFI National Archive](/wiki/BFI_National_Archive "BFI National Archive"), and is listed as one of the [British Film Institute](/wiki/British_Film_Institute "British Film Institute")'s "[75 Most Wanted](/wiki/BFI_75_Most_Wanted "BFI 75 Most Wanted")" lost films.[[4]](#cite_note-4)[[5]](#cite_note-BBClost-5)

## Production

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=A_Study_in_Scarlet_(1914_British_film)&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Production")]

[Ward Lock & Co](/wiki/Ward_Lock_%26_Co "Ward Lock & Co"), original publishers of *[A Study in Scarlet](/wiki/A_Study_in_Scarlet "A Study in Scarlet")* in the November 1887 edition of *[Beeton's Christmas Annual](/wiki/Beeton%27s_Christmas_Annual "Beeton's Christmas Annual")*, had the comprehensive rights to the book.[[6]](#cite_note-bostrom153-6) The company sold the film rights to [G. B. Samuelson](/wiki/G._B._Samuelson "G. B. Samuelson") and his [film company](/wiki/G._B._Samuelson_Productions "G. B. Samuelson Productions").[[6]](#cite_note-bostrom153-6)

Samuelson's plans for the film were ambitious[[1]](#cite_note-bostrom155-1)[[7]](#cite_note-barnes-7) with outdoor scenes filmed at [Cheddar Gorge](/wiki/Cheddar_Gorge "Cheddar Gorge") in [Cheddar, Somerset](/wiki/Cheddar,_Somerset "Cheddar, Somerset"), [England](/wiki/England "England") doubling for [Utah](/wiki/Utah "Utah") in the United States.[[1]](#cite_note-bostrom155-1)

James Bragington was an employee of Samuelson's company and was cast purely due to his resemblance to [Sidney Paget](/wiki/Sidney_Paget "Sidney Paget")'s famous [illustrations of Holmes](/wiki/Sidney_Paget#The_Strand_illustrations "Sidney Paget").[[7]](#cite_note-barnes-7) Author [Alan Barnes](/wiki/Alan_Barnes_(writer) "Alan Barnes (writer)") theorizes that Bragington may have been the first actor to wear a [deerstalker](/wiki/Deerstalker "Deerstalker") on screen.[[7]](#cite_note-barnes-7)

The success of the film led Samuelson to make another Sherlock Holmes film two years later, *[The Valley of Fear](/wiki/The_Valley_of_Fear_(1916_film) "The Valley of Fear (1916 film)")*.[[1]](#cite_note-bostrom155-1)

## Cast

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=A_Study_in_Scarlet_(1914_British_film)&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Cast")]

* James Bragington as [Sherlock Holmes](/wiki/Sherlock_Holmes "Sherlock Holmes")
* [Fred Paul](/wiki/Fred_Paul "Fred Paul") as Jefferson Hope
* Agnes Glynne as Lucy Ferrier
* Henry Paulo as John Ferrier
* James Le Fre as Father
* Winifred Pearson as Lucy, A Child
* Unknown actor as [Dr. Watson](/wiki/Dr._Watson "Dr. Watson")
